items.mistake: Fitting Ridge or Lasso on unscaled features
items.fix: Standardize continuous predictors (e.g. StandardScaler) in a pipeline; leave the intercept unpenalized.
items.why: L1 and L2 penalties sum across coordinates—features with larger raw scale get penalized less per unit of effect.
items.example: Income in dollars vs age in years: without scaling, the penalty barely affects income's large-magnitude coefficient.

items.mistake: Choosing alpha by minimizing training error
items.fix: Select λ with cross-validation or a held-out validation set using a proper scoring metric.
items.why: Training loss always favors smaller λ (closer to OLS); validation error reveals the bias–variance sweet spot.
items.example: CV picks alpha=10 while training MSE is lowest at alpha=0.001—using 0.001 overfits the training fold pattern.

items.mistake: Expecting Lasso to keep all correlated predictors
items.fix: Use Ridge when retention of correlated groups matters; use Elastic Net when you need both sparsity and grouping.
items.why: L1's axis-aligned constraint tends to pick one member of a correlated set and zero the rest.
items.example: height_cm and height_in both measure height; Lasso may keep one and zero the other despite both being informative jointly.
